stories.headline: Republican-led House deals blow to Trump over Iran war
stories.summary: Lawmakers vote to block president from carrying out more strikes in Middle East without congressional approval

stories.headline: US House passes Iran war powers resolution in rare pushback against Trump
stories.summary: Four Republicans allied with Democrats to pass bill to rein in Trump, though it is likely to face a presidential veto.

stories.headline: US House votes to halt Iran war, in rebuke to Trump
stories.summary: In the 215-208 vote, four Republicans joined Democrats to pass the measure, which had failed in three previous attempts.

stories.headline: US announces new tariffs over forced labour concerns
stories.summary: It comes after the US Supreme Court struck down many of US President Donald Trump's previous duties in February.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon agree to implement ceasefire
stories.summary: Joint agreement announced by the US state department calls for Hizbollah to stop all attacks

stories.headline: Oil Declines After US Says Israel and Lebanon Agree Ceasefire
stories.summary: Oil slipped after three days of gains after the US said Israel and Lebanon agreed to a ceasefire, which would remove a key sticking point in talks between Washington and Tehran.

stories.headline: Trump threatens tariffs on 60 trading partners including UK and Canada over ‘forced labour’
stories.summary: Proposal for 10-12.5% levies, to also include EU, Taiwan and Australia, would allow US president to skirt court-imposed limitsDonald Trump has threatened tariffs of between 10% and 12.5% on 60 trading partners including the UK, the EU and Australia over alleged forced labour failures, in the latest attempt to revive his signature trade policy.The EU immediately hit back, saying it expected the US to respect the tariff deal it entered into last Ju

stories.headline: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg as ‘Russian Davos’ opens in city
stories.summary: Energy and military sites targeted as guests gather for economic forum where Putin is due to speak on FridayUkrainian drones hit energy and military sites in St Petersburg early on Wednesday, hours before international guests gathered for the city’s flagship economic forum, in a blow to Vladimir Putin.Several long-range drones crashed into oil storage facilities after Russian air defences failed to shoot them down. stories.headline: US cites forced labour concerns as grounds for new tariffs
stories.summary: USTR's proposal comes from a Section 301 unfair trade practices investigation designed to help rebuild Trump's tariffs.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill nine in Lebanon as Hezbollah fires rockets over border
stories.summary: The incidents strain a partial ceasefire agreed by Israel and the Lebanese armed group on Monday.

stories.headline: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg as Putin's flagship economic forum opens
stories.summary: Several long-range drones struck oil storage facilities near the city, days before Vladimir Putin is due to address the event.

stories.headline: One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport
stories.summary: Iran says the attack on Kuwait was in retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island.

stories.headline: Ukraine attacks St Petersburg as Russia hosts its own ‘Davos’
stories.summary: Ukraine launched a series of air strikes targeting an oil facility and a naval air base near St Petersburg late Tuesday.

stories.headline: Eight killed after drone hits bus in Russian-occupied part of Ukraine
stories.summary: The bus was hit in the eastern Donetsk region, an official says.

stories.headline: South Korea’s Left-Wing Wins Big in Local Vote in Boost for Lee
stories.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung’s party won a resounding victory in key local elections, piling pressure on opposition conservatives as they struggle to reinvent their party after the debacle of their former leader’s martial law decree.

stories.headline: Goldman’s Japan Chief Counts on New Leaders to Drive Growth
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs Japan president Hidehiro Imatsu is promoting younger executives at the firm as a merger boom, rising stocks and volatile bond yields fuel the most intense talent war in years.

stories.headline: In Alabama Ruling, Supreme Court Signals Limited Role for Federal Courts in Redistricting Fights
stories.summary: In an emergency ruling on Tuesday night, the court’s conservative majority gave a first glimpse into congressional district battles under a weakened Voting Rights Act.

stories.headline: ‘I Love It’: Trump Is Still in Favor of $1.8 Billion Payout Fund
stories.summary: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, said on Tuesday that the administration was “not moving forward with the fund, period,” after the plan drew bipartisan backlash.

stories.headline: Park Service Awards No-Bid Contract to Cover Bridge Statues in Gold
stories.summary: Originally estimated to cost $2.4 million, the government will now spend $5 million to restore the bronzes in time for Independence Day.

stories.headline: Diphtheria outbreak: residents of remote NT community say health clinic has no hand sanitiser

stories.headline: Lula says Brazil cannot ‘accept treatment’ after new US tariffs proposed
stories.summary: Brazil's president says he was surprised by the proposed 25 percent tariffs, which came amid signs relations improving.

stories.headline: Grab what you can while you can: The new reality in the South China Sea
stories.summary: After years of watching China create land to back its expansive claims, others are doing the same.

stories.headline: Ex-UK political aide and Biden cabinet secretary lead California governor primary vote count
stories.summary: Another Democrat, billionaire climate activist Tom Steyer, is in third place as the tally continues.

stories.headline: Shell pumped oil through Nigeria pipeline for years despite pollution evidence, documents show
stories.summary: The oil giant says the documents ignore the critical context of the complex operating environment at the time.

stories.headline: Man shot dead by FBI after taking hostages at California bank
stories.summary: Two hostages were released on Tuesday, and the remaining hostages released on Wednesday were unharmed, police said.

stories.headline: Trump-backed candidate in Iowa loses primary
stories.summary: Randy Feenstra's defeat could prove to be a case of the president's political instincts being off.

stories.headline: Indonesia's Prabowo fires head of free meals scheme plagued by poisonings
stories.summary: The centrepiece programme of Prabowo's government has left tens of thousands of school children ill.

stories.headline: Why Canada has generic Ozempic, and the US doesn't
stories.summary: Canada is the first G7 country to approve the lower cost weight-loss drugs, which are rolling out in pharmacies this month.

stories.headline: Costly fuel pushes more Indians to buy electric cars but challenges remain
stories.summary: High fuel prices are strengthening the case for EV adoption in the world's third largest auto market.

stories.headline: Senior official from Germany’s AfD meets top Kremlin associates
stories.summary: Executives hold talks with far-right party, which leads German polls and supports restarting gas flows through Nord Stream

stories.headline: Broadcom loses more than $250bn in market value as revenue forecast disappoints
stories.summary: Shares in chip company fall as much as 15% in after-hours trading

stories.headline: Asian Stocks Drop After Broadcom Outlook, Oil Dips: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian stocks fell alongside US equity-index futures following a weak forecast from Broadcom Inc. and after renewed US-Iran clashes weighed on sentiment.

stories.headline: Rupiah Near 18,000 Level Has Markets on Guard for Intervention
stories.summary: The Indonesian rupiah is closing in on a key psychological level, putting market watchers on watch for a stronger response from the central bank.

stories.headline: What to Know About the SpaceX IPO
stories.summary: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is poised to have the largest stock-market debut in history when it goes public later in June. SpaceX is aiming to raise $75 billion in the initial public offering, more than twice the previous record holder.

stories.headline: High Yields Lured UK Money From Cash Funds to Bonds Last Month
stories.summary: UK-based investors pulled money from cash funds and piled into bonds last month, enticed by surging yields, according to fund flow data compiled by Calastone.

stories.headline: CFTC Ends Decades-Old ‘Gag Rule’ in Enforcement Settlements
stories.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is ending an almost 30-year-old policy that forbade companies and individuals from airing their grievances or denying wrongdoing after they settled enforcement cases with the derivatives regulator.

stories.headline: Petco Slides as CEO Comments Show Turnaround Not Finished
stories.summary: Petco Health & Wellness Co. shares fell as much as 18% in postmarket trading after the company’s second-quarter forecast fell below consensus estimates, outweighing a better-than-expected first quarter including a return to positive revenue growth for the first time in nearly two years.

stories.headline: Nomura's Nanavati on Asia M&A Momentum, Strategy
stories.summary: Nomura Head of Investment Banking for Asia ex-Japan Jwalant Nanavati shares his perspective on how landmark listings like SpaceX could shape the appetite for mergers and acquisitions across Asia and beyond. He speaks with Haslinda Amin from the sidelines of 'Nomura Investment Forum Asia' in Singapore. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Five Below Slides After Profit Beat as Retailer Flags Consumer Hit Over Oil High Prices
stories.summary: Shares of Five Below Inc. tumbled on Wednesday after the discount retailer beat first-quarter earnings expectations and raised its full-year profit guidance but signaled caution over the consumer in the months ahead.

stories.headline: Inotiv, Breeder of Laboratory Animals, Files For Bankruptcy
stories.summary: Inotiv Inc., which breeds dogs and imports monkeys that are used in drug experiments, filed for bankruptcy in part on what it blamed are tighter rules on its US canine farm and tariffs on non-human primates raised in China.

stories.headline: Macy's CEO on Earnings, Brand Strategy and Outlook
stories.summary: Macy's CEO Tony Spring discusses the company's earnings, consumer spending trends and the outlook for retail sales with Romaine Bostick on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: London activists try to erect statue of jailed Palestinian leader
stories.summary: Activists have tried to erect a statue of Marwan Barghouti in London’s Parliament Square, but police intervened.

stories.headline: Real Madrid will bring Mourinho back if Perez is re-elected
stories.summary: Real Madrid president Florentino Perez says he will bring Jose Mourinho back as manager if he wins Sunday's election.

stories.headline: Gunfire erupts in Mogadishu before protests against Somali president’s rule
stories.summary: Ex-Somali PM Khaire accuses government forces of attacking him before planned antigovernment protests in Mogadishu.

stories.headline: Bangladesh’s top diplomat elected UN General Assembly leader in tight race
stories.summary: Khalilur Rahman elected as the president of the UNGA's 81st session, garnering 99 votes in secret ballot.

stories.headline: EU court partially sides with Meta in a blow to the European Commission
stories.summary: The EU's General Court has annulled Facebook Marketplace's gatekeeper designation under the Digital Markets Act — but the ruling is largely procedural, as the Commission had already stripped the platform of that status a year earlier.

stories.headline: Energy transport corridors to Europe boosted by AI and new digital systems
stories.summary: AI and new digital technologies are key to the urgently needed alternative energy trade routes through the Southern Gas Corridor and the Middle Corridor systems connecting the Caspian and Europe.

stories.headline: Germany seizes tonnes of cocaine in €500m bust, suspects arrested in Spain
stories.summary: Instead of cacao beans, officials found more than 400 packets wrapped in black foil, each containing about 20 blocks of compressed cocaine.

stories.headline: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
stories.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

stories.headline: New York police investigate videos of men emerging from city's sewer system
stories.summary: Authorities warn that entering the sewer system is both illegal and extremely dangerous.

stories.headline: Putin remains uncompromising on Ukraine, but is public discourse on war changing in Russia?
stories.summary: Russia is intensifying attacks in Ukraine but more than four years of war are causing concern even among Putin loyalists.

stories.headline: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble after Russian strikes kill 22 people
stories.summary: An eight-year-old boy killed in a strike on an apartment block are among the dead, officials say.

stories.headline: Sailors stressed and exhausted after months trapped by Strait of Hormuz blockade
stories.summary: The uncertainty has weighed heavily on the 20,000 seafarers trapped in the Iran war zone.

stories.headline: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
stories.summary: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
stories.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

stories.headline: Iran attacks damage 20 US military sites since start of war, satellite images show
stories.summary: Analysts told BBC Verify that Tehran's strikes were more extensive than publicly acknowledged.

stories.headline: Hungarian PM threatens to oust Orbán-era president
stories.summary: Hungary's president has refused Prime Minister Péter Magyar's demands to step aside, setting up a constitutional clash.

stories.headline: Poland's controversial 'Highway to Hel' 666 bus revived
stories.summary: Poland's seaside resort called Hel is getting its 666 bus service back, despite earlier opposition from Christian groups.

stories.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

stories.headline: Caribbean hot sauce producers warn of shortages and higher prices
stories.summary: Manufacturers in Jamaica say the key chilli peppers they need are in limited supply.

stories.headline: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
stories.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu calls the capture of the strategic fortress a "decisive shift" in Israel's campaign against Hezbollah, as European governments criticise the escalation.

stories.headline: Ecuador accused of meddling in Colombian election with tariff vow
stories.summary: Ecuadorian leader Daniel Noboa offers to cancel tariffs after speaking to a right-wing presidential candidate ahead of Sunday's vote.

stories.headline: Netanyahu says he has directed IDF to increase control of Gaza to 70%
stories.summary: The expansion in control by Israel would contradict the terms of the ceasefire  Israel and Hamas agreed to in October 2025.

stories.headline: US and Iran 'very close' to deal but 'not there yet', Vance says
stories.summary: US officials earlier told the BBC that the framework of a ceasefire extension deal had been agreed, pending the approval of Trump and Iran's leadership.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.
